finding: tests/test_agent_router.py and tests/unit/coding_control_plane/test_agent_router.py share a basename with no __init__.py. pytest's default prepend import-mode cannot import both, so COLLECTION ABORTED and THE ENTIRE SUITE NEVER RAN. Each file passes in isolation.
consequence: This is why 85 test files were 'never run'. It was never a discipline problem. A single filename collision made the whole suite un-runnable, and nothing surfaced that — the run just errored out and everyone moved on. Verification blindness, not laziness.
